item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs another round of proofreading, particularly the Containment Strategy Summary.

I'd make the edits, but there is just enough doubt in my mind that they could possibly be intentional. Possibly meant to indicate Taylor Hunt wrote this quickly, without taking the time to get the grammar perfect.

And if edits are made, I'd also like to suggest some variation in terminology. "Burial" and "buried" show up way too often. The thesaurus is not a kind of dinosaur :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's more the Creon and Pharoah events being the notable events they would've noticed. Then looking back and attempting to figure out the connecting factors they would find a lack of burial records, and then experiments would find the Antigone events. Not to mention the appearance of tombstones that never existed before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: right, i understood the bit about area-1444, i was mostly confused about how the foundation was even detecting new headstones and burials before the creation of that area. not really a huge deal though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made proofreading edits to fix this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The containment procedures are written from a perspective of the status quo, where all the Antigone Events bury the bodies at a singular location (Area-1444). Thusly, there's no real need to track them as of now, since they just need to monitor Area-1444 for new burials.

In the past, the answer is they weren't, really. Burials were occurring all over the place and it was very hard to track them. The effect on normalcy wasn't big, because nobody really cared about the people being buried (that's why 1444 buries them) and a few new headstones in a cemetery isn't the end of the world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i dig this, especially the implications at the end.

one thing i'm confused about is the appearance of these un-buried bodies. are they from everywhere in the world? how is the foundation capable of tracking every dead body that doesn't get buried? or are these supposed to be so few and far between that it's easy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. The preponderance of people becoming interns after they die kept me from being able to take this seriously. Inter, interred, interment, those are the words you need.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You use the verb "interned" in a few places. Unless these corpses are doing unpaid corporate work, I'm guessing you meant "interred", as in "placed in a tomb or grave".

Edit: Now I'm seeing a lot of uses of "inter" too and I'm extra confused.

It also feels like the first line of the description is missing a verb: "SCP-1444 is an anomalous phenomenon affected human corpses (designated SCP-1444-1) that would otherwise not undergo any form of burial or interment."

I feel like this needs some major proofreading. There's a ton of errors in spelling and grammar.

item.title: A Gravedigger for the Forgotten
item.content:encoded:
This has been in my sandbox for a long time. As in, since mid-July of 2017. I never really worked on it for a long time, but it was in my head for a while. I knew exactly what I wanted to do with it, I just needed to put words to paper. And then a Series II slot opened up with a number I liked, I didn't have anything ready to post and I saw this in my sandbox. I slammed this out real quickly - it just flowed.

Anyway, thanks to drnelson3, captainkirby and Baubius for looking this jam over.
